seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience.
new_constraint: Write the email in an informal tone, tailored for a casual audience.
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience. Write the email in an informal tone, tailored for a casual audience.
response:
Here are two versions of the email as requested.

### Option 1: Professional & Respectful (Academic Audience)
**Subject:** Extension Request: [Project Name] – [Your Full Name] – [Course/Section ID]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this message finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully request a short extension on the [Project Name], which is currently due on [Date]. Due to an unexpected circumstance regarding [briefly state reason, e.g., a personal family matter / a technical issue with my research data], I have been unable to finalize all components of the project by the original deadline.

I recognize that you have many submissions to review, and I do not wish to inconvenience your grading schedule. However, I believe granting a brief extension until [Proposed New Date] would allow me to submit a complete and high-quality assignment rather than a rushed one. If an extension is not possible, I understand if you require me to submit a partial draft or work toward recovering lost points through other course assessments.

Thank you for your time and understanding. I have attached any relevant documentation supporting my request.

Sincerely,

[Your Full Name]
[Student ID Number]

***

### Option 2: Casual & Informal (Casual Audience)
**Subject:** Quick question about the [Project Name] deadline 👋

Hi [Professor's First Name],

Hope you're having a good week!

I'm reaching out because I've run into a bit of a rough patch trying to wrap up the [Project Name] and I'm worried I might not be able to get everything polished off in the next 48 hours. Something came up unexpectedly that has thrown off my timeline a little.

Would you be open to moving the deadline back by maybe a day or two? I want to make sure I hand something over that I'm actually proud of, rather than just rushing it at the last minute. If you're really swamped, no problem at all—I can send over what I have so far instead.

Thanks so much for considering it, and let me know what works best for your schedule!

Best,

[Your First Name]<|im_end|>
